item.title: New York enacts nation's first statewide moratorium on building new data centers
item.description: Building new data centers in New York will be paused for a year in order to allow state officials to establish guidelines protecting residents and the environment, Gov. Kathy Hochul said.

item.title: Scammers are using FaceTime to pose as banks and steal your money. Here's what to watch for.
item.description: Consumer advocates say they're tracking a rise in cases where scammers are using FaceTime. The scams often start with a text about suspicious activity on your bank account or credit card. Ash-har Quraishi explains.

item.title: U.S. citizens traveling in Congo must spend 21 days elsewhere before entering U.S.
item.description: U.S. health officials are concerned about the spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Inflation eased more than expected in June, CPI report shows
item.description: Lower gasoline prices slowed inflation in June, though many household costs remained stubbornly high.

item.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Maine was not intended target of warrant, lawmakers say
item.description: Maine Sen. Angus King said he told Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin that he wanted a transparent investigation into the shooting in Biddeford.

item.title: ICE killing of Joan Sebastian Guerrero sparks demands for investigation
item.description: Joan Sebastian Guerrero's death in Maine marks the second time in a week that Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have shot and killed a civilian. The Department of Homeland Security alleged that a man "attempted to flee the scene" when stopped by ICE. Guerrero's killing has prompted calls for an investigation. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: New report details Israel's apparent secret plan to cultivate Iran's Mahmoud Ahmadinejad
item.description: The New York Times published an explosive report on Israel's apparent secret plan to cultivate former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as an intelligence asset. Ronen Bergman, one of the authors of the report, joins CBS News with more details.

item.title: Apple files lawsuit against OpenAI over trade secrets apparently stolen
item.description: Apple is suing OpenAI over apparently stolen trade secrets that may be linked to hardware ambitions. CBS News contributor Patrick McGee joins with more.

item.title: Oil prices surge as Trump suggests U.S. fees on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump's declaration that the U.S. will control the Strait of Hormuz and charge fees on cargo has sparked a second day of oil price rises.

item.title: Maps show extreme heat and flood threats across U.S.
item.description: Much of the U.S. is facing either extreme heat or excessive rainfall and potential flooding on Tuesday. Here's where the greatest threats are expected.

item.title: Wildfires are roaring near Paris, forcing evacuations
item.description: Extreme weather conditions are keeping dangerous wildfires alive near Paris, France. CBS News' Chris Livesay reports.

item.title: Witnesses describe moments after ICE officer fatally shot Joan Sebastian Guerrero
item.description: Witnesses are describing what they saw during the moments after an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent fatally shot a man in Maine. One source with knowledge of the investigation says Joan Sebastian Guerrero was killed during the encounter. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Astronomers discover sugar in space that's also found in raspberries
item.description: The sugar, called erythrulose, lurks in what's called the interstellar medium: thin clouds of gas and dust littered between stars.

item.title: At least 30 killed in Bangkok beer hall fire, authorities say
item.description: Video shared by first responders​ shows a huge blaze, with flames coming out of the front door of the Na Ladprao bar in the northern part of the Thai capital.

item.title: Can mortgage rates fall without another Fed rate cut? Here's what experts think
item.description: There's a chance mortgage rates could fall even if the Fed continues to hold its benchmark rate steady. Here's how.

item.title: Astronomers find sugar in space that is usually in raspberries and self-tanners
item.description: Astronomers have found a type of sugar, called erythrulose, in space. CBS News' Errol Barnett reports.

item.title: Judge's stunning rebuke of Trump, DOJ over IRS lawsuit: "Brought for an improper purpose"
item.description: U.S. District Judge Kathleen Williams slammed President Trump, his lawyers, and the Justice Department over a lawsuit against the IRS, saying it was filed for an "improper purpose." CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: Sen. John Thune gets emotional during speech honoring Lindsey Graham
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune honored the late Sen. Lindsey Graham on Capitol Hill following his sudden death. CBS News' Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: Man describes harrowing escape from wildfire that killed wife and friends
item.description: A last minute decision meant Malcolm Timbrell survived one of Spain's deadliest wildfires ever, but his wife and friends were trapped by a wall of flames.

item.title: More U.S. strikes slam Iran as tensions roar over Strait of Hormuz and Bab al-Mandab waterways
item.description: The U.S. launched strikes in Iran for a third consecutive night, and tensions over traffic in the Strait of Hormuz grow. CBS News' Aaron Navarro and Aaron MacLean have more.

item.title: Folarin Balogun says he knew suspension of red card would "cause a lot of controversy"
item.description: U.S. soccer star Folarin Balogun spoke to "CBS Mornings" about the red card controversy during the World Cup, saying while he didn't feel the red card was justified, he knew suspending it "would cause a lot of controversy." Balogun discusses how he navigated the situation and the U.S. performance in the tournament.

item.title: Trump says Reflecting Pool has been drained for repairs
item.description: President Trump said the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was drained for repairs, after weeks of railing against alleged vandals.

item.title: U.S. uses sea drones in combat for first time to attack Iranian port
item.description: The U.S. military shared video of what it said was its first use of sea drones in combat, to attack an Iranian submarine and ship maintenance facility.

item.title: Astronomer's name deciphered from ancient Mayan ruins for first time
item.description: The name "White-chested Fox" was found in drawings dating from 400 BC to 900 AD at the San Bartolo-Xultun archaeological site.

item.title: Why gaining full control of Strait of Hormuz has proven so hard for U.S.
item.description: Restoring oil tanker traffic in the vital Middle East shipping corridor to prewar levels likely will require a much bigger armada of U.S. warships if not tens of thousands of American troops on Iranian soil, experts say.

item.title: Climbers found dead in Italian Alps week after going missing
item.description: The two climbers had not made contact since leaving a mountain refuge on July 9, according to authorities.

item.title: States call out big businesses with employees on Medicaid
item.description: With the federal Medicaid work requirement looming in January, Democrats are considering state legislation to call out big companies that employ workers enrolled in the safety net health program.

item.title: Maps show heat alerts as extreme temperatures threaten millions
item.description: Heat alerts were issued for millions across parts of the western U.S. Sunday due to an unusually prolonged heat dome, which is starting to move east.

item.title: Video shows NBA's Brandon Clarke stopped by police 6 weeks before his death
item.description: Arkansas police said they found bags of capsules containing a green powdery substance in Brandon Clarke's car, which he told them was kratom.

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI, accusing ChatGPT maker of stealing trade secrets
item.description: Apple alleges that OpenAI and two of its employees stole trade secrets and engaged in a "pattern of misconduct."
